tweets.text: You have options before choosing to voluntarily abandon a prohibited item. You can take it out to your vehicle, leave it with a non-traveling companion, or place it in a checked bag. Best option is to not bring these items in your carry-on. Always start packing with an empty bag. https://t.co/EHJSwIliDh

tweets.text: Reminder: Passengers who engage in unruly behavior at the checkpoint or inflight may face substantial penalties and possible prosecution on criminal charges. @FAANews has more info regarding inflight disturbances here: https://t.co/TfFviNWRwC https://t.co/bxAXB3bbFS
tweets.text_resolved: Reminder: Passengers who engage in unruly behavior at the checkpoint or inflight may face substantial penalties and possible prosecution on criminal charges. @FAANews has more info regarding inflight disturbances here: https://www.faa.gov/unruly https://t.co/bxAXB3bbFS

media.alt_text: No threats. No verbal abuse. No physical violence of any kind. If you threaten, endanger, or harm Transportation Security Officers during screening, TSA will pursue criminal penalties and fines of up to $17,062. Thank you for your cooperation.

tweets.text:
Your bag is packed, but is it checkpoint ready? 👀

Before you head to the airport, take 30 seconds to #PackSmart: 
✅Double-check your bag for anything prohibited (like this camping stove) 
✅Have your ID with you and ready
✅Empty your pockets

Your future self will thank you. https://t.co/O65IUnMp99

tweets.text:
A traveler approaches TSA certain they removed any knives from their carry-on.

The X-ray determines… that was a lie.

Because beyond the boarding pass and tangled charger cables lies a dimension of forgotten multitools and misplaced confidence.

A place called… #TheCarryOnZone https://t.co/18nW75wv2n

tweets.text: We’re 48 days out from @FIFAWorldCup. If you need info on traveling into the U.S., reach out to @CBP. For more info on international travel policies or visa processing, @StateDept has your answers. @TSA will be your midfielder for any domestic travel questions. https://t.co/JeNygAvYa5

author.description: This is the official X account of the Transportation Security Administration (TSA).

tweets.text: What does "empty your pockets" mean? TSA's definition: to take out everything in one's pockets before screening begins. It doesn't matter which airport you're at or the time; place your items in your bag and carry-on faster than the rest. https://t.co/1wICz8m5Y4
tweets.text_resolved: What does "empty your pockets" mean? TSA's definition: to take out everything in one's pockets before screening begins. It doesn't matter which airport you're at or the time; place your items in your bag and carry-on faster than the rest. https://t.co/1wICz8m5Y4

author.description: This is the official X account of the Transportation Security Administration (TSA).

tweets.text:
A few things we've made happen during this Golden Age of Travel:

✅Easier travel for families through dedicated lanes at select airports.

✅Benefits for military & uniformed service members.

✅Ended the "shoes off" policy.

All while maintaining the highest security standards. https://t.co/N6YUZrZVwn
tweets.text_resolved:

author.description: This is the official X account of the Transportation Security Administration (TSA).

tweets.text:
Not sure if it’s a liquid? If you can spill it, spray it, spread it, pour it on your salad, or dip your pizza in it… it should be 3.4 oz or less in a carry-on bag.  

More questions that need a-dressing? Reach out to @AskTSA. https://t.co/SjH7jXAEay
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
REAL ID or passport = smoother travel 

However, if you arrive at the airport and don’t have an acceptable form of ID (or if you’ve had it stolen while on travel), you can use TSA ConfirmID to verify your identity and travel domestically. 

Learn more: https://t.co/f48R2xL4pM https://t.co/xiXRHtKgnC

tweets.text: Your safety is always our priority. Respecting our teams as they make that happen is a must. TSA officers work hard to keep the skies safe. Planning to fly soon? Make sure you have the right ID for security screening BEFORE arriving at the airport. Acceptable forms of ID include: 

✅ REAL ID
✅ U.S. passports
✅ Military ID
✅ DHS trusted traveler cards

For the complete list, visit: https://t.co/iyRhK8hASn https://t.co/qXdYnPLXtx

media.alt_text: TSA 2025 by the numbers - groundbreaking initiatives that have enhanced the traveler experience and reinforced our commitment to the Golden Age of travel - 94% REAL ID compliance rate since May 7, 2025 - TSA PreCheck 1300+ enrollment centers - 30+ dedicated families on the fly, serve with honor, and travel with ease lanes to make traveling stress free - 9.4+ million TSA PreCheck Touchless ID verifications processed - 99% of passenger get through security in 10 minutes or less - 47+ million people eligible for TSA PreCheck screening - AskTSA responded to 3.3 million questions within 5 minutes - TSA Cares help line assisted 78,000 travelers - TSA Contact Center assisted 2.7 million calls and email messages.

author.description: This is the official X account of the Transportation Security Administration (TSA).
This is the official X account of the Transportation Security Administration (TSA).

tweets.text:
We've talked a lot about REAL ID and TSA ConfirmID lately, & here's a question we're seeing:
Do children need a REAL ID to fly?
Short answer: No. TSA doesn't require minors to provide an ID when traveling domestically.

For more details on ID requirements: https://t.co/drmICHMz7D https://t.co/uNA7HEnXsw
tweets.text_resolved:
We've talked a lot about REAL ID and TSA ConfirmID lately, & here's a question we're seeing:

For more details on ID requirements: https://www.tsa.gov/ID https://t.co/uNA7HEnXsw

tweets.text:
What if you're traveling domestically and your ID is lost or stolen? TSA ConfirmID is your backup, using secure tech to verify your identity if you don’t have REAL ID-compliant identification. For a $45 fee, it's valid for 10 days of travel. 

Learn more: https://t.co/8RMsLuq0Lz https://t.co/05TVncL3Rw
